notes: Major ext4 features merged since ext4 forked from ext3. Seed commits identified from EXT4_FEATURE_INCOMPAT_/RO_COMPAT_/COMPAT_ macro additions; the pipeline derives merged_date, merged_version, and Fixes-tag counts automatically.

features.name: Extents and 64-bit
features.description: INCOMPAT_EXTENTS and INCOMPAT_64BIT, ext4's two defining feature bits over ext3: extent-mapped allocation instead of indirect blocks, and 48-bit physical block addressing.

features.name: Multi-block allocator
features.description: Buddy-allocator-style multi-block allocator replacing ext3's single-block allocator. Required for usable performance once extents arrived.
features.notes: Authored by Alex Tomas / Aneesh Kumar. Merged v2.6.25.

features.name: Metadata checksums
features.description: RO_COMPAT_METADATA_CSUM adds CRC32c protection on inodes, block group descriptors, journal blocks, and other metadata.
features.notes: Authored by Darrick Wong. Merged v3.5.

features.name: Inline data
features.description: INCOMPAT_INLINE_DATA stores small file contents inside the inode body, skipping a block allocation for tiny files.
features.notes: Authored by Tao Ma. Merged v3.8.

features.name: DAX support
features.description: Direct access to persistent memory, mirroring the XFS DAX work. Shared most of its mm/ infrastructure with the XFS path.
features.rhel_notes: Tech Preview earlier, then supported. Later deprecated.
features.mm_impact_notes: Same mm/ refactor as XFS DAX: devmap pages, pgmap accounting.
features.notes: Authored by Matthew Wilcox. Merged v4.0.

features.name: Encryption (fscrypt)
features.description: INCOMPAT_ENCRYPT adds per-file content and filename encryption with policies stored as extended attributes. ext4 led; later generalized as fscrypt.
features.mm_impact_notes: Touched the page-cache read path for transparent decryption.
features.notes: Authored by Michael Halcrow and Theodore Ts'o. Merged v4.0.

features.name: Case-folded directories
features.description: INCOMPAT_CASEFOLD stores a charset encoding in the superblock so directory lookups can be performed case-insensitively without touching VFS.
features.notes: Authored by Gabriel Krisman Bertazi. Merged v5.2.

features.name: fs-verity
features.description: RO_COMPAT_VERITY adds Merkle-tree-based file integrity verification, designed for read-only system images on Android.
features.mm_impact_notes: Hooks in page-cache read path for Merkle tree verification.
features.notes: Authored by Eric Biggers. Merged v5.4.

features.name: Fast commit
features.description: INCOMPAT_FAST_COMMIT adds a small per-fsync log next to the main journal that records only the changes needed to recover the most recent operations, dramatically reducing fsync latency.
features.rhel_notes: Not exposed as a supported feature.
features.notes: Authored by Harshad Shirwadkar (Facebook). Merged v5.10.

features.name: Orphan file
features.description: COMPAT_ORPHAN_FILE replaces the in-superblock orphan inode list with a dedicated file, fixing a long-standing serialization bottleneck on truncate/unlink-heavy workloads.
features.notes: Authored by Jan Kara (SUSE). Merged v5.15.

features.name: iomap conversion (direct I/O)
features.description: Direct I/O path lifted to iomap, replacing the legacy ext4 DIO machinery and improving large-IO performance.
features.mm_impact_notes: Page-cache awareness of iomap on direct I/O path.
features.notes: ext4 buffered I/O remained on buffer_head until very recently. Merged v4.10.

features.name: Multi-fsblock atomic writes
features.description: Atomic write support for ext4 using bigalloc, exposing the device's atomic write unit through the statx unit-max-opt field.
features.notes: Authored by John Garry (Oracle). Merged v6.16.

features.name: Large Block Sizes (LBS)
features.description: Block sizes larger than PAGE_SIZE on ext4. Lands after XFS once the underlying mm and iomap large-folio infrastructure was already in place.
features.first_idea_note: Christoph Lameter's compound-page RFC predates ext4's split from ext3 and motivated the entire LBS arc.

historical_attempts.author: Christoph Lameter
historical_attempts.note: Compound-page support for large block sizes in the page cache.

historical_attempts.author: LSFMM 2017
historical_attempts.note: LSFMM discussion on whether LBS was worth revisiting.

features.mm_impact_notes: Shares the mm and iomap large-folio infrastructure that LBS-on-XFS forced into existence. ext4 also picked up the iomap buffered I/O path for the LBS path.
features.notes: Authored by Luis Chamberlain (kernel.org) and others. Merged v6.19. The mm cost was already paid by the XFS LBS work; ext4 inherited the runway.
